mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at 4.48% combined with rising CPI (317.685) signals monetary tightening cycle. Elevated real rates compress equity valuations and increase discount rates, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as market reprices growth expectations downward over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:September 2008 financial crisis onset with elevated policy rates
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields fall in > 55% of instances when trigger is activated, or Rank IC < 0.05 over rolling 12-month windows during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.1% represents tight labor market. Sustained low unemployment signals wage pressure and inflation persistence, forcing the Fed to maintain or raise rates, which increases Treasury yields as long-term rate expectations adjust upward.
evidence_refs: fact:Low unemployment in mid-2008 before crisis acceleration
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: Treasury yields decline in > 60% of 20-day forward periods despite UNRATE < 4.5%, or correlation between UNRATE and 10Y yield changes falls below 0.10 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1289k units combined with retail sales (RSAFS) at 724.6B dollars indicate demand-side weakness emerging. Declining demand signals recession risk, causing flight-to-quality into Treasuries and yield compression as growth expectations fall over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month OR RSAFS growth < 0.1% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market deterioration in 2008 preceding financial crisis
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 50% of triggered instances despite demand weakness signals, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window in demand-contraction regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 101.96 index level reflects supply-side capacity utilization. Declining industrial production signals economic slack and deflationary pressure, causing Treasury yields to fall as inflation expectations compress and real rates adjust downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Manufacturing weakness in 2008 financial crisis period
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 55% of instances when INDPRO declines, or Rank IC < 0.06 over rolling 12-month windows during supply contraction periods.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 159,536k persons combined with unemployment rate at 4.1% creates a divergence signal. If payroll growth stalls while unemployment remains low, it signals labor market deterioration and imminent recession, causing Treasury yields to fall sharply as safe-haven demand increases.
evidence_refs: fact:September 2008 employment deterioration during financial crisis
regime_hint: labor market shock and recession acceleration
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 60% of triggered instances, or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in > 70% of recession-onset periods.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 23,400.3B dollars (last available quarterly) combined with declining housing starts signals demand destruction. Weakening aggregate demand forces the Fed to eventually cut rates, causing long-term Treasury yields to fall as the yield curve steepens in anticipation of monetary easing.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 8% quarter-over-quarter OR GDPC1 growth rate < 1.5% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:Q2 2008 GDP growth slowdown and housing crisis
regime_hint: demand-driven recession with policy accommodation expected
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 55% of triggered instances despite recession signals, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year window in demand-contraction regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at 4.48% represents elevated policy stance relative to inflation. If CPI growth decelerates while rates remain high, real rates become restrictive, signaling policy error. Market reprices long-term growth expectations downward, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as recession probability increases.
evidence_refs: fact:Monetary policy tightening bias in mid-2008 despite emerging crisis
regime_hint: monetary policy tightening bias with inflation deceleration
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 60% of triggered instances, or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in > 65% of periods with this rate-inflation divergence.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 101.96 combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 159,536k shows supply-labor coordination. Simultaneous weakness in both signals indicates broad-based economic contraction, causing Treasury yields to fall as investors flee to safety and growth expectations collapse.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < -0.3% AND PAYEMS month-over-month change < 0
evidence_refs: fact:Synchronized manufacturing and employment weakness in 2008 financial crisis
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 55% of triggered instances despite synchronized supply-labor weakness, or Rank IC < 0.07 over rolling 12-month windows during contraction periods.
